intro: Voyria alba, also known as White Voyrie, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Voyria alba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Voyria alba is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve coughs and soothe respiratory tract issues.
